---
subject: "What should I ask Katja Hoyer?"
from: "Marginal REVOLUTION <donotreply@wordpress.com>"
to: ""
date: 2026-03-01 15:51:49
labels: ["CATEGORY_PERSONAL", "INBOX", "Tyler Cowen", "UNREAD"]

Yes I will be doing a Conversation with her. She is the author of a forthcoming book on Weimar, namely [Weimar: Life on the Edge of Catastrophe](https://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=86d7ca5b90091e0f7d8924ecfe0605b54e0a77789ebc20fc926a357f22e22f9b&blog_id=42693868&post_id=92536&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=b02fa5f6cdbd7654c07a7ba90b660b4f&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=b72f08919f859bdbac2c0b586a7640e9). Note that much of the book considers the city of Weimar, mostly in Nazi times, and not just the Weimar era. She also has published [Beyond the Wall: A History of East Germany](https://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=801907a2d63c65dfe10c9256e0de4343071604ac17e78e14c22db6bf29b486fc&blog_id=42693868&post_id=92536&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=02e6773a4c85d42eae11aa628a8c3298&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=b72f08919f859bdbac2c0b586a7640e9), and [Blood and Iron: The Rise and Fall of the German Empire 1871-1918](https://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=bce49dd2aea34abf59f2c7ff36426eee58c193a982f29bc912a9752698e810e9&blog_id=42693868&post_id=92536&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=842ee6f4f0d1ecf8e98850e278993d5f&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYW1hem9uLmNvbS9CbG9vZC1Jcm9uLVJpc2UtR2VybWFuLUVtcGlyZS9kcC8xNjM5MzYyOTc1L3JlZj10bW1fcGFwX3N3YXRjaF8w&email_id=b72f08919f859bdbac2c0b586a7640e9). She is active in journalism, podcasting, and is a visiting research fellow at King's College London. She was born in East Germany and is both British and German.
So what should I ask her?
